primary_field: code surfaces Knight has touched + architectural decisions across sessions

adjacents.field: librarian-mcp/ MCP server and indexer architecture
Firebase hosting targets and GCP project layout
test discipline — node --test, try/finally, serial chaining

provenance: Seeded K461/B121 from Knight's architectural observations across K436-K462 sessions. Covers major code surfaces, naming conventions, and design patterns Knight has established.

observation: librarian-mcp/src/indexer/ — Knight's conventions: 13-stage buildIndex.ts pipeline. Each stage is a named parseX() function returning a typed index object; writeIndex() serializes to JSON with size logging. Incremental mode: checkFreshness() against SHA-256 tree-hash fingerprint; FRESH = skip all 13 stages; DRIFT = re-run all stages but use cached results where possible. Stage 9 (parseDropzones) covers BISHOP_DROPZONE, KNIGHT_DROPZONE, ROOK_DROPZONE, PAWN_DROPZONE. Stage [14/13 added K461]: Knight Cathedral Courier — runs after stage 13, appends-only to knight_cathedral/scribes/.

librarian-mcp/src/scribes/ — Cathedral I/O primitives (K436). Four files: cathedral.ts (file-system I/O, STITCHPUNKS_DIR env override for tests), consult.ts (retrieval), fates.ts (Three Fates routing pipeline), registry.ts (Scribe expert registry). STITCHPUNKS_DIR env var: before importing cathedral.ts, set this env var to redirect all I/O to a temp dir — this is the test isolation primitive for mutating tests. Knight's Cathedral adds knight_cathedral/ as a sibling directory in stitchpunks/; cathedral.ts itself is not modified.

platform/supabase/ — Knight's conventions: migrations use YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_description.sql naming. Pedestal tables live in upekrithen schema (NOT public), no FK to public.members (separation enforced at schema level). RLS append-only: upekrithen.pedestal_issuance_log has no UPDATE/DELETE via RLS. user_id fields reference auth.users, NOT members. Cathedral tables (K438a): cathedral.* schema with 5 tables, RLS, append-only invariants, auto-provisioning trigger, starter-pack seeder.

platform/src/components/ and platform/src/pages/ — Knight's conventions: WildFire Tour mode gating (K454): mock data ONLY shown when tour mode is active, never in Real Data default state for new users. New routes follow the pattern of React component + /route-path. DSS portal runs from platform/src/DSSApp.tsx via hostname detection, NOT from dss-the2ndsecond/ legacy folder. All portal domains share one build (npm run build in platform/); only Cephas has a separate build step.

librarian-mcp/ test discipline (established K447-K462): all tests use node --test with native Node.js test runner. Mutating tests (tests that write files) must: (1) own node --test invocation chained with &&, (2) use try/finally to restore original state, (3) run serially vs other mutating tests. Non-mutating tests can be grouped in one node --test invocation. LIBRARIAN_STITCHPUNKS_DIR env var is the sandbox isolation primitive for tests touching Scribe files.

librarian-mcp/src/server.ts — MCP tool registration pattern (K448): use registerTool() wrapper (not raw server.tool()) for all tool registrations. Wrapper handles build-lock gate, type-casting, and structured error responses. Post-K448 hygiene: grep for leftover `server.tool(` should return 0 (all registrations migrated). MCP server lines 2323-2344: Cathedral tools (log_tidbit, fates_route, scribe_log, consult_scribes) registered.

canonical_values.yaml discipline (K456/K460): ONLY hand-edit source for canonical counts. Downstream files (overview.json, useCanonicalStats.ts DEFAULTS for five fields) are auto-generated on npm run rebuild. Never hand-edit useCanonicalStats.ts DEFAULTS for innovationCount, crownJewels, patentApplications, patentClaims, productionSystems — those lines are overwritten on next rebuild. Drift check: npm run verify:canonical fails loudly on mismatch.

sessions.json — Knight's architecture clarification (K462): it is a live MCP-written store, NOT a build artifact. The indexer reads it but never writes it. Location: gitignored (not committed). update_session MCP tool writes it with plausibility guard (K460): IDs >9999 hard-rejected; IDs exceeding prefix max by >200 rejected; TEST_ escape hatch for legitimate test use. Session format: {id: string, summary: string}; canonical ID pattern: letter-then-digits (e.g., K461, B121).

librarian-mcp/stitchpunks/ — append-only JSONL architecture: all tablet files are append-only. First line = header (type: 'header'). Subsequent lines = entries. The log_tidbit / scribe_log MCP tools enforce append-only at the write path. Filesystem-level append atomicity is the durability substrate — tablets accumulate growing, immutable records. Knight's Cathedral adds knight_cathedral/ as a peer of scribes/ and data/ directories. Any correction to an existing tablet must append a new tablet with a supersedes field pointing to the prior tablet's timestamp — never edit in place.

KNIGHT_QUEUE.md — Phase 1 to Phase 2 transition (K461): Phase 1 was Bishop-maintained manually at session close. Phase 2 (this session) makes it a derived view rendered by librarian-mcp/scripts/render-knight-queue.mjs from KnightQueue.jsonl + KnightHandoffs.jsonl. NEXT/QUEUED/LANDED sections auto-render on npm run rebuild. CONTEXT section remains Bishop-maintained (not auto-derived). File lives at workspace root (c:/Users/Administrator/Documents/LianaBanyanPlatform/KNIGHT_QUEUE.md).

MAIN world fetch intercept pattern (K508 reusable): To intercept and modify outgoing page fetch() calls in a Chrome MV3 extension — (1) injected.js declared in manifest with world: MAIN, run_at: document_start; (2) override window.fetch, parse body, postMessage({__namespace: request, requestId, query}) to window; (3) content.js (ISOLATED, also document_start) receives via window.addEventListener(message), calls chrome.runtime.sendMessage, posts response back; (4) injected.js resolves Promise keyed by requestId, rebuilds request with modified body. MAIN world scripts cannot use chrome.* APIs — postMessage bridge is mandatory.

Comet Bridge extension architecture change K485A→K508: librarian-mcp-helm-pwa/comet-bridge-extension/ — v0.1.0 used DOM-replacement (content.js keydown intercept, setQueryText, refire Enter). v0.2.0 uses network-intercept (injected.js MAIN world fetch override + content.js postMessage bridge). background.js unchanged except lastIntent now saved to storage. manifest.json adds injected.js entry with world:MAIN, run_at:document_start; content.js moved from document_idle to document_start.

Local retry queue for BRIDLE Rule 4 eventual-consistency. MCP tools: reminder_scribe_query_history + reminder_scribe_drain_retry. 10/10 tests pass. commit: b59b20b

ReminderScribeMetrics.tsx React dashboard (anti-shame, rolling-window filter, FORK alert banner, drift indicators). MCP tool reminder_scribe_metrics_query. 9/9 tests pass. Pod-I PIPELINE COMPLETE. commit: 65c2391

title: KN-J1 House Scribe Core � Population-Ratio Audit + Jar Lifecycle
summary: House Scribe long-term archive keeper at population-scale. population_audit.ts: cron-class ratio audit (1 HS per 10K Pheromone records; configurable). jar_lifecycle.ts: 4-state machine created->indexed->sealed->retrievable. Jar sealing = STRUCTURALLY-IMMUTABLE (forever-stamp class). Cathedral-prefixed HS serial (LB-{CAT}.HS-NNNN) extended in provenance_chain.ts. Supabase migration: jars_of_honey + jar_events + RLS + structural-immutability trigger. 4 MCP tools: house_scribe_create_jar / house_scribe_seal_jar / house_scribe_query_jars / house_scribe_population_audit. T1-T10 + 2 INFRA = 12/12 pass. Commit 1c253e7.
